danger_signals.visual_form: Helicopters, tanks, police cars visible on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to player; enemy fire incoming

danger_signals.visual_form: Bombs, dynamite visible in broken windows
danger_signals.meaning: Damaging hazard; will harm monster if collected
Damage on contact or activation; dangerous food source
Flash blinds monster temporarily, causes fall damage if triggered

danger_signals.visual_form: George, Lizzie, or Ralph visible and attacking
danger_signals.meaning: Punch damage; direct collision threat in multiplayer

safety_signals.visual_form: Fruit, roast chicken visible in broken windows
Currency or point indicators in windows

safety_signals.meaning: High point reward when grabbed; beneficial interaction

safety_signals.visual_form: Special item at Plano, Illinois (end of cycle)
safety_signals.meaning: Full health restoration for all monsters; major point bonus

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Helicopters, tanks, police cars, uniformed soldiers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Helicopters fire projectiles; tanks shell from ground; police cars pursue; soldiers attack on foot
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military vehicles patrol cities; soldiers positioned in buildings

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Human NPCs in windows, pedestrians, photographers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Photographer blinds with flash; some civilians neutral or harmful; taxi drivers attack
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Civilians visible in windows; concentrated in populated building interiors

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: George (King Kong gorilla), Lizzie (Godzilla reptile), Ralph (werewolf)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Three distinct monster character palettes with different silhouettes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Monsters occupy buildings; visible climbing and destroying infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cities appear standard with intact buildings; military presence moderate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies engage at normal aggression; civilians present in buildings; destruction flows naturally

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Building density increases; varied building layouts in different cities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Military forces intensify; enemy variety expands (helicopters, tanks, dynamite traps)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mega vitamin bonus item appears; special visual marker for final level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Full health restoration triggered; major score multiplier; cycle resets after consumption

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Monster transforms into original human character; walks off screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player loses current score; can be eaten by other monsters; vulnerable on ground

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blimp descends to screen carrying monster; full health bar displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player returns to monster form; scores reset but gameplay continues

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: City names change; starting in Peoria IL, ending in Plano IL; cycle repeats 5 times
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: 28 distinct cities across North America (avoiding 7 specific states); identical building mechanics

environmental_storytelling.technique: Window contents reveal hazards and resources
environmental_storytelling.example: Broken windows show bombs (danger), food (safety), photographers (traps), cigarettes (dual-purpose). Player learns to distinguish safe vs. harmful items through interaction
Opening sequence explains George (scientist with vitamins), Lizzie (radioactive lake contamination), Ralph (hot dog food additive). Establishes 'monster-as-victim' narrative

environmental_storytelling.technique: Military escalation in response to destruction
environmental_storytelling.example: Helicopters, tanks, police, and artillery increase presence as buildings are destroyed. Environmental response signals player actions have consequences
Intact buildings → rubble piles → cleared zones. Visual accumulation of destruction communicates player impact on environment
128-day journey across North America (Peoria to Plano) positions rampage as continental event; 7 spared states creates alternate-reality USA
Waving NPCs signal distress (point multiplier); photographers actively blind player; soldiers fight back. Each NPC type communicates different world-state response

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Climbs buildings, punches destructively, engages enemies
Reverts to human form; walks off screen vulnerable
Scales buildings, attacks with reptilian aggression
Fires projectiles downward at monsters; persistent threat while on screen
Shells buildings and monsters from ground level; immobile threat
Hands waving; high point multiplier if grabbed quickly
Must be eaten before flash triggers; flash causes temporary blindness and fall damage
Consumable for health; represents military presence inside infrastructure

notes: Rampage (NES 1988) is a destruction-based action game with minimal narrative complexity but strong implicit world-state communication through hazard/resource visual distinction and military-vs-monster conflict. The game lacks explicit faction diplomacy but establishes clear opposition: player monsters vs. military/civilian resistance. Visual manifestation relies heavily on window contents (safe/dangerous items), military vehicle presence (threat level), and building destruction progression (environmental impact). The geographic 128-day cycle across North America creates implicit political world-building: a multi-state monster rampage treated as inevitable disaster rather than invasion. No explicit dialogue or cutscenes beyond opening sequence. Environmental storytelling primarily through interactive destruction and NPC behavior variety.
